items.question: In marketing, what is the primary purpose of a brand?
items.correctAnswer: To influence perceptions so customers understand what a company offers and what differentiates it.
incorrectAnswers: To replace pricing entirely with discounts
To guarantee customer satisfaction with no effort from the company
To automatically prevent competitors from entering the market
items.explanation: A brand helps people interpret and remember an organization’s value, positioning, and consistency.

items.question: What does brand positioning describe?
items.correctAnswer: How a brand is positioned in customers’ minds relative to alternatives.
incorrectAnswers: The exact manufacturing process used to make the product
Only the company’s legal name and corporate structure
The monthly payroll schedule for marketing staff
items.explanation: Positioning explains what the brand stands for and why it is different from competitors in a clear customer-facing way.

items.question: What is a brand promise?
items.correctAnswer: A clear statement of the value or experience customers can expect from the brand.
incorrectAnswers: A list of all company job roles and responsibilities
A promise that every product feature is the cheapest in the market
A guarantee that returns will always be denied
items.explanation: A brand promise sets expectations, so the company must deliver consistently to build credibility.

items.question: What is brand equity, in practical terms?
items.correctAnswer: The added value a brand gives to products or services because of recognition, trust, and associations.
incorrectAnswers: A legal document that only covers trademarks
The current stock price of the company’s parent business
A guarantee that marketing spend will always increase demand
items.explanation: Brand equity reflects customer and market perceptions that can make demand, pricing, and loyalty more favorable.

items.question: What is the difference between brand identity and brand image?
items.correctAnswer: Identity is what the company intends to communicate; image is how customers perceive the brand.
incorrectAnswers: Identity is only colors and fonts, while image is only the company’s mission statement
Identity and image always match exactly in every market
Identity is internal and image is legal, not customer-facing
items.explanation: Identity is the planned message and cues; image is the real perception shaped by experiences, messaging, and word of mouth.

items.question: Why is a logo not the same thing as a brand?
items.correctAnswer: A logo is a visual symbol; a brand includes meanings, associations, and customer experiences.
incorrectAnswers: A logo is always irrelevant to marketing decisions
A logo legally defines company culture for all employees
A brand is only the colors used on a website
items.explanation: Brand is broader than a mark—customers form beliefs from many touchpoints, not just the visual identity.
